statement.markdown: Given a positive integer n, return the number of set bits in its binary representation.
statement.outputFormat: Return the number of set bits in the binary representation of n.

examples.explanation: The binary representation of 5 is 101, which contains 2 set bits.
The binary representation of 7 is 111, which contains 3 set bits.

hints.text: This problem is a classic example of bit manipulation.

editorial.title: Incorrect Count of Set Bits
editorial.intuition: Use the fact that bitwise AND operations can help us count set bits.
editorial.bruteForce: This problem is typically solved using a binary counter.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use bitwise shift and bitwise operations to count set bits.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Prove that the optimal solution is correct using mathematical induction.

starterCode.javascript: var countBits = function(n) { // TODO: implement; return 0; }

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution {public: int countBits(int n) { int ans = 0; while (n != 1) { ans += n & 1; n /= 2; } return ans; };}
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution {public int countBits(int n) { int ans = 0; while (n != 1) { ans += n & 1; n /= 2; } return ans; };}
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def countBits(self, n: int) -> int: ans = 0; while n != 1: ans += n & 1; n //= 2; return ans;
buggyStarterCode.javascript: var countBits = function(n) { var ans = 0; while (n != 1) { ans += n & 1; n /= 2; } return ans; }

solutions.python: class Solution: def countBits(self, n: int) -> int: ans = 0; while n > 0: ans += n & 1; n >>= 1; return ans;
solutions.cpp: class Solution {public: int countBits(int n) { int ans = 0; while (n) { ans += n & 1; n >>= 1; } return ans; }; return 0; }
